Party_Details.Name: Ho Pak Kim Realty Co Pte Ltd (in liquidation)

Facts.Text: HPK was incorporated with main activities in civil engineering and real estate development
D1 and D2 were directors of HPK since its incorporation
Revitech served statutory demands on HPK for an outstanding sum of $1.619 million
HPK was wound up on 27 October 2017, and Don Ho was appointed as its liquidator

Party_Details.Issue: Whether directors are legally responsible for failing to collect debts owed to a company in liquidation
Party_Details.Rule: Directors must act in the best interests of the company and its creditors, especially when the company is insolvent
Party_Details.Application: The Defendants did not collect the $3.59 million debt owed to HPK by the related parties
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: The court found that the Defendants breached their duties by not pursuing the debt, which was a significant asset of the company, and acted against the interests of the company's creditors.

Party_Details.Name: Ho Soo Fong

Facts.Text: Ho Soo Fong was a director of HPK since its incorporation
Ho Soo Fong signed HPK's 2012 Financial Statement, which showed that related parties owed HPK $3,590,587 as at 2012-08-31
Around 2016-12-21, Ho Soo Fong instructed workers to discard HPK's documents after CAD seized some documents

Party_Details.Issue: Whether a director is legally responsible for failing to act in the company's best interests by not collecting debts
Party_Details.Rule: Directors must exercise reasonable diligence and act in the best interests of the company and its creditors
Party_Details.Application: He was unaware of the company's financial situation and relied on others for management. the Defendant's inaction led to the company's inability to pay its creditors
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: The court held that the Defendant failed to exercise reasonable diligence and did not act in the best interests of the company, contributing to its financial difficulties.

Party_Details.Name: Ho Soo Kheng

Facts.Text: Ho Soo Kheng was a director of HPK since its incorporation
D2 signed HPK's 2012 Financial Statement, which showed an amount owing from related parties of $3.59 million as at 31 August 2012

Party_Details.Issue: Whether a director is legally responsible for failing to act in the company's best interests by not collecting debts
Party_Details.Rule: Directors must exercise reasonable diligence and act in the best interests of the company and its creditors
Party_Details.Application: He was a silent partner and unaware of the company's financial situation. the Defendant's inaction led to the company's inability to pay its creditors
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: The court found that the Defendant failed to exercise reasonable diligence and did not act in the best interests of the company, contributing to its financial difficulties.
